QUID for CMEMS OMIs:  
IBI_OMI_WMHE_mow 
Ref: 
Date: 
Issue: 
CMEMS-IBI-QUID-005-mow 
15 April 2020 
1.2 
 
 
    
 Page 3/ 5 
 
Product name: IBI_OMI_WMHE_mow 
Indicator evaluation method:  
The Mediterranean Outflow Water (MOW) interannual variability is monitored in the defined boxes by 
computing the salinity anomaly. Salinity data is extracted from several CMEMS systems and averaged in 
each monitoring domain. Anomalies of timeseries of salinity have been computed referenced to  the 
monthly climatology obtained from IBI-MYP.  
The index is computed from the following CMEMS products: 
• IBI-MYP: IBI_REANALYSIS_PHY_005_002 
• IBI-NRT: IBI_ANALYSIS_FORECAST_PHYS_005_001 
• GLO-MYP: GLOBAL_REANALYSIS_PHY_001_025 
• CORA: INSITU_GLO_TS_REP_OBSERVATIONS_013_001_b 
• ARMOR: GLOBAL_REP_PHY_001_021 
Results of diverse products are combined to deliver a unique multi -product result. Multi-year products 
(IBI-MYP, GLO-MYP, CORA, and ARMOR) are used to show an ensemble mean and the standard deviation 
of members in the period covered by them. The short -range product, IBI -NRT, is not included in the 
ensemble, it is used to provide the deterministic analysis of salinity anomalies in the most recent year. 
Scientific validation: 
The selected monitoring areas aim to provide a reliable representation of MOW variability. A bigger 
reference box identified as the MOW core Reservoir (as it is described in Daniault et al., 1994). And other 
different smaller boxes were selected as representative areas  in view of their specific variability of 
currents and salinity properties (Pascual et al., 2018). A climatic study of salinity sections along latitudes 
(45º N, 40º N, 35.9º N, and 30.6º N) and longitudes (15º W, 10º W, 8º W, and 5º W) together with 
bibliographic support (Prieto et al, 2013) has led to define 1000 m as the reference MOW core depth. 
The quality evaluation of IBI_OMI_MWHE_mow is based on the coherence of results with a set of other 
CMEMS products.The index is estimated with two IBI products IBI-MYP and IBI-NRT in the period 1992-
2018 (Figure 1), however in the figures it is included a shaded area computed from the spread of a group 
of other CMEMS gridded products (GLO-MYP, CORA, and ARMOR).  
 
The quality evaluation of IBI_OMI_MWHE_mow is based on the coherence of results with a set of other 
CMEMS products. The index is estimated as an ensemble product (Figure 1) for the period covered by 
the multi-year products (IBI-MYP, GLO-MYP, CORA, and ARMOR). In in the timeseries of the indicator it 
is included a shaded area representing the spread (computed as the standard deviation) of the ensemble 
members. The uncertainty of the result is assessed by the size of the area defined by the ensemble 
spread. The assim ilation of ARGO data in CMEMS systems leads to an increment of the coherence 
between different products after 2002. Therefore, the higher discrepancies can be found in the years 
previous to the assimilation of ARGO data in CMEMS systems. This is especially  remarkable in the 
monitoring boxes Reservoir, South, and West. On the contrary, the widespread agreement between IBI 
and other CMEMS products after 2004 implies a good reliability of results during the ARGO period. More 
details and full scientific evaluation can be found in the CMEMS Ocean State Report (Pascual et al., 2018) 
and Pascual-Collar et al. 2019.

catalog_entry.summary: This document is a Quality Underpinning Information Document (QUID) for the Ocean Monitoring Indicator of Mediterranean Outflow Water (MOW) from the Copernicus Marine Environment Monitoring Service, detailing its methodology and validation process.

product_line.name: Mediterranean Outflow Water (MOW) Ocean Monitoring Indicator
